sglang.title: Support min-p sampling
sglang.author: intervitens
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

#1071
Modifications

Implemented the min-p sampling algorithm using both flashinfer kernels and the native pytorch sampling implementation. There is a slight slowdown when using min-p due to the current lack of a fused min-p/top-p/top-k kernel in flashinfer. To avoid this slowdown when m

Rename /v1/health to /health_generate for the health check in #1154. The endpoint name "/v1" are for openai APIs so we do not want to use it for SRT endpoints.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Mirror jinja2 in pyproject.toml
vllm.author: sasha0552
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In #7695, jinja2 was added to requirements-build.txt but was not mirrored in pyproject.toml, causing build errors in a clean environment such as cibuildwheel.
https://github.com/sasha0552/pascal-pkgs-ci/actions/runs/10485853952/job/29042838246

vllm.title: [BugFix] Avoid premature async generator exit and raise all exception variations
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The async generator returned by AsyncLLMEngine generate() uses a queue of either request outputs and/or exceptions.
But the logic consuming requests from the queue only raises instances of Exceptions, not Exception classes (which are also raisable in python). This means that the CancelledError class

vllm.title: [Spec Decoding] Use target model max length as default for draft model
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We currently assume a hard-coded default of 2k for the max model length in the case that it can't be inferred from the model's configuration.
In the case of spec decoding draft models, it would instead be better to assume the same max length of the target model in this case.
This is particularly hel

vllm.author: sasha0552

vllm.title: [Frontend] Improve Startup Failure UX
vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

currently, when the RPCServer fails during initialization, we throw a RuntimeError and a TimeoutError on the RPCClient and crash, alerting the user that we died during startup
this is confusing, because the useful source of the error is far up in the stack trace
this PR instead logs an ERR

Utility PR to add torch profiler support to vllm worker, openai client, and benchmark_serving.py.
Enabled and configured through env vars:
VLLM_TORCH_PROFILER_DIR=/mnt/traces/

vllm.title: [Bug][Frontend] Improve ZMQ client robustness
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Related to #7394
Followup for #6797
This PR adds some client-side tests for the zmq client that interacts with the async engine process, and adds timeouts for all of the connections. This ensures that we won't end up deadlocked waiting for a server response that never comes.

vllm.title: [TPU] Avoid initializing TPU runtime in is_tpu
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#7659 broke the TPU backend with TP > 1 because xm.xla_device(devkind="TPU") tries to initialize the TPU runtime for TPU 0 before the worker processes are assigned the rank and set their devices. This leads to a deadlock.
This PR fixes it by just using libtpu as a proxy for checking whether the mach
